page: Microsoft Azure NC8ads_A10_v4 is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine belonging to the NCADSA10v4 family. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor using the x86_64 architecture. The virtual machine runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, ensuring dedicated CPU allocation for workloads requiring consistent compute performance.

This server configuration includes 100.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 12.5 GB of RAM per core. Hardware acceleration is provided by a single NVIDIA A10 GPU with 22 GB of dedicated VRAM. For local data storage, the instance is equipped with a 737 GB local disk and is premium storage capable, allowing for high-speed disk operations.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the hardware profile balances dedicated AMD compute cores, a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, and a dedicated GPU accelerator on a single virtual machine. This resource density makes it suitable for GPU-accelerated workloads, machine learning inference, data analytics, and graphics-intensive applications that require substantial local storage and memory capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure NC8ads_A10_v4 is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine from the NCADSA10v4 family, designed for workloads requiring dedicated compute and graphics acceleration. It features 8 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by an AMD processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is equipped with 100.0 GB of system memory, providing 12.5 GB of RAM per core, and includes a single NVIDIA A10 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM. For storage, the instance provides a 737 GB local disk and supports premium storage configurations. This hardware profile delivers a balanced combination of dedicated CPU resources, high memory capacity, local storage, and GPU acceleration, making it suitable for machine learning inference, data processing, and graphics-intensive workloads.
